Transcrição Completa: 5 Personal Principles And Anchors

[00:00] So, I want to talk to you about this concept called anchors. And I'm going to relate it to friends. Because no matter where you're at in life, you can always identify people in real time that might be holding you down or might be pushing you up to different levels depending on where you are, who you're associated with, what your ideals are, what values you have.

[00:23] There's a lot of stuff that we can talk about when it comes to personal principles of how you're associated with the people called friends in your life. Now, I want you to understand something. And as I've evolved as an entrepreneur, as I've seen, studied, and identified the habits of billionaires, millionaires, and everybody that I look up to as mentors, they've all been able to cut people off in real time that are of no benefit to their gains.

[00:49] And anybody that holds them back in real time, they understand this powerful quote, change is the only constant in life. And your ability to apply that constant into your life. And stay dynamic, stay nimble, don't remain static in a particular area, including your friend list in real life.

[01:09] Who are your associations? Why are they associated with you? What are they doing for you? You know, are their goals dynamic? Are they holding you down? Are they keeping you up?

[01:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Even Playing Field - Average"

NÚMEROS: N/A

VISUAL: Linha horizontal representando o nível médio/average, campo de jogo nivelado, ponto de referência zero

[01:23] So, check this out. Let's say right here is the even playing field, a solid, you know, level, if you will, called average. Now, let's say that as you are going through life, you continue to self-educate. You continue to maybe advance your career if you don't have as, you know, aggressive entrepreneurial goals as some other viewers may have.

[01:45] You build that business you want to build. You grow your agency to the next level. You do whatever is necessary in order to grow above where you were today. Now, do me a favor. Toss me a red marker real quick.

[01:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Upward Trajectory vs Downward Spiral"

NÚMEROS: N/A

VISUAL: Linha verde ascendente representando crescimento pessoal, linha vermelha descendente representando declínio de amigos, separação visual entre as duas trajetórias

[02:01] Now, let's say you have friends and other people in your life that hold you back and keep you down and put you in a position where as you go up, they continue to do drugs and they continue to sit their ass on a couch and not move their life forward or they continue to work those low paying jobs selling time instead of selling value.

[02:24] Now, there's an interesting universal law that exists that I want to fill you in on, and it's this concept called voids. Now, voids don't exist. They're those empty spaces. Have you ever seen a fish in a fishbowl? They're surrounded by water, right?

[02:38] But yet, you know, they obviously breathe somehow in the water and they survive in the water. Yet, do you think all this stuff around you right now is just simply empty? Or do you understand it to be the same concept from a perception standpoint as the fish in water?

[02:54] Do you perceive that we have an atmosphere around us that's filled with a particular set of compounds that we're breathing in right now and that we're in right now just like a fish in water? Do you have the perception to agree that there's no such thing as a void?

[03:10] So technically as a separation occurs, you can even break it down to a molecular level. You can break it down to a subatomic level. There's no such thing as a void. Yet almost everything's a void.

[03:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Void Concept - Universal Law"

NÚMEROS: N/A

VISUAL: Representação da separação crescente entre duas linhas, conceito de espaço vazio que não pode existir, analogia do peixe na água

[03:25] So what does that mean? When we're talking about friends and anchors and Jeremy, what are you talking about? How does this have to do with leveling up? As this separation occurs, see how this line exists here on this simple graph?

[03:38] Well, the way it really looks is that at certain times as your friends continue to go down, all their weight, literally all of it, continues to pull them down and it continues to go down further past the point it was initially at.

[03:53] Yet, you are going to continue going up. And as you continue to go up and your weight continues to prosper past the point you're currently at now, all of a sudden you have a big issue on your hands.

[04:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Rubber Band Effect"

NÚMEROS: N/A

VISUAL: Ilustração de elástico sendo esticado entre duas pessoas, tensão crescente, ponto de ruptura iminente, representação visual da separação forçada

[04:07] It's almost like when you have a rubber band and your friends across the room and you pull it and you pull it and you pull it and it gets tighter and you damn well know that thing's going to snap and you're almost wondering, do you let it go faster than the other person's going to let it go?

[04:22] And you know that when it gets to a certain point of stretch, you're like, oh god, they're going to let go. It's going to hurt. Jeez. And then sometimes the other person gets it, but sometimes it comes back to you and it snaps and it hurts every single time.

[04:35] But it comes from not recognizing that there's that separation that occurs. And as soon as an empty space, as soon as a void starts to exist, guess what happens? Snaps right back. Doesn't exist.

[04:47] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Billionaire Decision Making - Ray Dalio Example"

NÚMEROS: $100 bilhões (valor da empresa de Charles Co/Ray Dalio)

VISUAL: Referência a líderes bilionários, conceito de "thoughtful disagreement", ambiente de corte rápido de relações, hierarquia de decisões empresariais

[04:50] You are either going to make the cut like many billionaires, hundred millionaires. Do you know Charles Co who built a hundred billion dollar company is known for making some of the quickest cuts from having what he considers an environment where thoughtful disagreement is encouraged where these you know environments of disagreeing openly with other people can lead you to being more dynamic in the relationships you have that you need at the right moments at the right times to fulfill self-interest which let's be honest is what most of us are doing at all points anyway within our entire lives for why we're in relationships anyway unless you're truly selfish and most of us are selfish.

[05:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Pull Back Scenario - Worst Case"

NÚMEROS: N/A

VISUAL: Seta descendente vermelha forte, representação de ser puxado para baixo, perda de potencial, destruição de autoconfiança, afundamento abaixo da linha média

[05:38] You may get stuck some instances as that rubber band continues to go out. Somebody says, "Hey, whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa. This whoa. This is going to snap. Hold on. Come back. Come here. Come closer. Let's put the rubber band. Come here." And you get closer. And you get closer. And then the worst thing happens. The absolute worst possible scenario occurs.

[05:59] You get pulled right back down and you actually get affected in real time. And it's the worst possible thing to observe because rather than you going anywhere and getting anything done for the direction that you were headed, you actually start to get pulled down and your potential not only becomes limited, it decreases and your self-confidence gets destroyed and all of a sudden you pass that average medium of what is now within their reality.

[06:28] And now as they continue to go down and as they continue to anchor you and as the ocean floor gets deeper, you continue to go down with them because you didn't have the capacity to cut off an anchor when you needed to. That was sinking your ship.

[06:44] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Critical Decision Point"

NÚMEROS: N/A

VISUAL: Ponto de decisão crítico, âncora afundando navio, necessidade de cortar relações tóxicas, representação de afundamento no oceano

[06:47] You have to cut people off when you need to. Otherwise, you'll have some crazy scenarios occur like rubber bands as you let go, either snapping the other person, and nobody likes to get snapped. That doesn't help anybody.

[07:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Personal Story - Ages 3 and 10 Friends"

NÚMEROS: 2 melhores amigos, idades 3 e 10 anos de amizade

VISUAL: História pessoal de confrontação sobre uso de drogas, tentativa de intervenção, consequências dramáticas

[07:03] Did you know at one point I'd actually called two of my best friends from ages three and 10 out on doing drugs after helplessly saying, "Hey, I'm simply trying to figure out talking to my grandfather every day. Oh, what can we do? What can we do? What can we do? I need help."

[07:19] And the moment that I call it out and try to bring the situation to light, oh boy, that rubber band snapped. Boom. came right back at me. Hurt really bad. Actually almost died. It was an attempted murder on my own balcony and it was me.

[07:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Life-Threatening Consequence"

NÚMEROS: 1 tentativa de assassinato

VISUAL: Situação extrema na varanda, quase morte, momento de percepção crítica, intervenção salvadora de terceiro

[07:38] I'd actually had my entire life go to the point of non-existent. Thankfully, there was somebody in the house who had hollered to let me go because obviously I was going to die. At that point, I realized this very truth and I was able to identify it many other scenarios in life where you could either once again safely come back, drop the rubber band, you could safely let the other rubber band go, let it snap back on the other person and keep moving forward so aggressively that you're never going to feel the pain from those other individuals.

[08:14] Or you in real time could wmp wmp w drag yourself down if you don't have the ability to cut off anchors. This is how to level up. you need to level up and you need to cut the anchors in your life.

[08:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Action Item - Assessment Exercise"

NÚMEROS: 2 colunas (positivo/negativo)

VISUAL: Sistema de cores verde e vermelho/azul e vermelho, coluna negativa destacada em vermelho, lista de pessoas, avaliação individual

[08:31] Go ahead as an action item and assess each person in your life. Do a little positive and a little negative column. I like to do it with two typical colors that are great for color psychology such as maybe a green and a red, a blue and a red. Definitely use red. That negative column just got to be lit up with all the anchors in your life.

[08:51] Just go ahead one by one list them off. And if you have a lot, I also need you to know you really have a lot to think about outside of just cutting off anchors. You have to think why do you have so many people in the red?

[09:04] But that's a different lesson on how to level up. This has been lesson three. I'll see you in the next video.

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

$100 bilhões - valor da empresa citada (Ray Dalio/Charles Co)

2 melhores amigos perdidos (desde idades 3 e 10 anos)

1 tentativa de assassinato sofrida

Lição 3 da série "How to Level Up"

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Conceito de Âncoras: Pessoas que te puxam para baixo vs pessoas que te elevam

Lei Universal dos Vazios: Não existe espaço vazio - quando há separação entre crescimento pessoal e declínio de amigos, algo tem que ceder

Efeito Elástico: Três cenários possíveis quando há separação:

Você volta voluntariamente (pior cenário)

O elástico estoura no outro (doloroso mas necessário)

Você se afasta tão rápido que não sente o impacto

Exercício de Avaliação: Sistema de duas colunas (verde/positivo e vermelho/negativo) para avaliar cada pessoa na vida

Filosofia Bilionária: Cortes rápidos e decisivos de relações improdutivas

Thoughtful Disagreement: Ambiente de discordância construtiva usado por bilionários

ESTRUTURA:

Linha Base: Conceito de "average" como ponto de partida

Trajetórias Divergentes: Crescimento pessoal ascendente vs declínio de amigos descendente

Três Resultados Possíveis:

Corte limpo e avanço

Retorno forçado e destruição de potencial

Snap back que machuca ambos

Princípio do Navio e Âncora: Metáfora de afundamento quando não se corta a âncora

Psicologia de Cores: Verde/azul para positivo, vermelho para negativo

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

Referência a empresa de $100 bilhões como exemplo de liderança que faz cortes rápidos

Conceito de "selling value" vs "selling time" (trabalhos mal pagos)

INSIGHTS PESSOAIS:

História real de quase morte após confrontar amigos sobre uso de drogas

Salvamento por terceiro que estava na casa

Momento de percepção crítica que mudou perspectiva sobre relacionamentos

CALL TO ACTION:

Avaliar cada pessoa na vida usando sistema de duas colunas

Identificar âncoras (coluna vermelha)

Refletir sobre por que existem tantas pessoas negativas (se aplicável)

Cortar relações tóxicas antes que arrastem você para baixo

DURAÇÃO: ~9 minutos FORMATO: Vídeo educacional com apresentação visual (quadro branco/marcadores), storytelling pessoal e frameworks conceituais
